Significant difference. The build with analyzers takes ~18.5s, without analyzers ~9s. That's roughly a 2x improvement.

Now let me also check for the solution configuration - 6 platform configurations (Debug|Any CPU, Debug|x64, Debug|x86, Release|Any CPU, Release|x64, Release|x86), all building for "Any CPU". The x64 and x86 configurations map to "Any CPU" which is wasteful. 

Let me also look at the redundant project references and check if there's a `Directory.Build.targets` or `.editorconfig`.

1. MSBuild evaluation/orchestration (21,622ms in MSBuild target - 32 calls)
2. `_GetProjectReferenceTargetFrameworkProperties` - 9,589ms (5 calls) 
3. Redundant project references causing excessive graph traversal

This is a small codebase (5 projects), so the real-world time isn't huge, but the proportional overhead from redundant references, analyzers during dev loop, documentation generation, and unnecessary platform configurations is significant.


Let me check if there's a setting to disable transitive project references, then run a graph-based build to see if that helps reduce the redundant reference overhead.

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Here's my analysis and recommendations:

---

## Build Performance Analysis

### Baseline Measurements
| Configuration | Time |
|---|---|
| Full build (with analyzers + docs) | **~18.5s** |
| Without analyzers/docs | **~9s** |
| With `-graphBuild:true` | **~3s** |
| Actual compilation (Csc) | **~0.4s** |

The actual C# compilation is only **425ms** — over **97% of build time is overhead**.

---

### Issues Identified

#### 1. `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true` always on (~9s overhead)
In `Directory.Build.props`, code style analysis runs on every build, even during the inner dev loop. This doubles build time and also causes the build to fail (via `TreatWarningsAsErrors`) on style issues like `IDE0160`.

#### 2. `GenerateDocumentationFile=true` always on
XML doc generation for every build is wasteful during development.

#### 3. Redundant transitive project references (causes excessive MSBuild graph traversal)
- **BusinessLogic** → references `DataAccess` + `Common` (Common is already transitive via DataAccess)
- **WebApi** → references all 3 lower projects (only needs BusinessLogic)
- **Tests** → references all 4 projects (only needs BusinessLogic or WebApi)

This caused `_GetProjectReferenceTargetFrameworkProperties` to take **9.5s** with 32 MSBuild calls for a 5-project solution.

#### 4. No graph-based build enabled
`-graphBuild:true` isolates project evaluation and avoids redundant re-evaluations — it reduced build time from 18s to 3s.

#### 5. Unnecessary solution platforms
6 configurations (Debug/Release × Any CPU/x64/x86) all map to `Any CPU` — the x64/x86 platforms add no value.

---

### Recommendations

I'll now implement the fixes:

    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Analyzers always on, even for dev inner loop -->

    <AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591;CA1000</NoWarn>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Excellent! Graph-based build reduces from ~18s to ~3s! That's a massive improvement. This is because the graph build avoids re-evaluating the same project multiple times (the redundant project references cause Common to be evaluated 4+ times, DataAccess 3+ times, etc.).

Now let me summarize the key findings:

1. **Build baseline**: ~18.5s with all analyzers, ~9s without analyzers, ~3s with graph build
2. **Key issues**:
   - `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true` + `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true` in `Directory.Build.props` - forces full style analysis on every build (adds ~9s)
   - Documentation generation is always enabled even during development, and there are redundant transitive references throughout the project structure where BusinessLogic pulls in both DataAccess and Common when Common is already transitive, WebApi unnecessarily references all three projects when only BusinessLogic is needed, and Tests references all four when it only needs WebApi and BusinessLogic
   - The solution lacks graph-based build optimization and has six configurations that all map to the same platform, creating unnecessary complexity
   - Analyzer rules are scattered across MSBuild properties instead of being centralized in an `.editorconfig` file

, which forces all analysis through the MSBuild pipeline

Now I'll work through the specific optimizations and provide recommendations.

data.content: The issue is that `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` needs to be explicitly set to `false` for Debug. Let me fix this:

arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>
    <NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591;CA1000</NoWarn>
    <!-- Disable expensive operations during dev inner loop; CI/Release overrides below -->
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>false</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>false</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Full analysis and doc generation for CI/Release builds -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)' == 'Release' or '$(CI)' == 'true'">
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
